title: CDR & Tower Triangulation Timeline
description: Reconstruct a device's geographic movement from Call Detail Records and cell-tower data. Play the timeline to animate the device between towers, click CDR rows to locate towers, and show the trilateration that estimates a position from three tower ranges.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Mobile Forensics
Cell Phone Analytics
Call Detail Records
Trilateration
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — use CDR and tower data to reconstruct device movement.
chapter_title: Cell Phone Analytics and Mobile Forensics

educational.topic: Reconstructing device movement from CDR and cell-tower geometry
learningObjectives: Use CDR rows (time, tower ID) to order a device's tower contacts.
Reconstruct geographic movement by mapping tower contacts onto a coverage map over time.
Apply trilateration: estimate a location from three tower ranges and state its uncertainty.
educational.prerequisites: Reading a table; basic coordinate geometry; the idea of a coverage radius

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Map + table + timeline: play the timeline to animate the device along its tower path, click CDR rows to highlight the tower, and toggle a trilateration overlay showing three range circles converging on an estimated location
pedagogical.assessment: Students order the tower contacts, trace the path, and interpret the trilateration estimate and its uncertainty radius
pedagogical.modelType: Discrete tower-contact path animation plus a geometric trilateration overlay (three circles intersecting at the estimate)
controls: click a CDR row to locate its tower
